rows.indicator: white and yellow and blue license plates
rows.description: The UK uses white front, and yellow rear plates. Some feature the EU blue left side however this isn’t always the case.

The UK often will have reflectors in between the center dashes on roadways. There are many different style of road lines that can ultimately be found. You will also find the famous “Zebra Crossing” nearly everywhere. You will even find that these crossings contain some lights for night time safety.

Britain within the UK uses a system by which to number it’s roads. A roads are usually main roads, and B roads are usually smaller. The numbers seen on this map are always second in the naming, e. A380.

rows.indicator: light poles actually nearly completely replace the cross walk sign seen in most
rows.description: The light poles actually nearly completely replace the cross walk sign seen in most countries.
(Welsh). (English). (Scottish). The UK uses proper English, while Wales has a mix of Welsh and English and Scotland can have some Scottish along with English.
This bollard has a very large red reflector on the top.
The UK seems to love these row houses. If it looks similar to the houses in Harry Potter, its a good tell.
The UK uses wooden Electricity poles that look like these.
Signs in the UK can be white, green and blue. Look for the letters A or M, as these are the major roads throughout the country.

UK mobile numbers begin with 07 in the format of: 07xxx xxxxxx. Most major cities have their own area code, with some big ones to remember being:. (0141) Glasgow. (0151) Liverpool. (0161) Manchester. (020) London. (028) North Ireland.

The UK can vary between its individual countries by look, however most urban areas look similar, the main differences occurring in more rural regions, Scotland being the most mountainous, followed by Wales, then England, and Northern Ireland. The UK will often have large hedges on the both sides of rural roads.

rows.indicator: UK also has a post code system which can help you narrow down which part of the
rows.description: The UK also has a post code system which can help you narrow down which part of the country you are in.

rows.indicator: You will also see these yellow warning signs on most poles
rows.description: You will also see these yellow warning signs on most poles. They show a guy getting zapped by a lightning bolt.

warnings: Local deterministic extraction used GeoTips text only; associated images were not interpreted, so descriptions contain no image-derived details.
